reforms.description: A portion of corporate profits over a certain level will be transferred to a special investment fund managed by the Central Bank, designated for productive investment and requiring agreement with employee representatives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages reinvestment of corporate profits into productive activities, potentially enhancing overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The measure was included in the general price freeze and related economic policies.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how corporate profits are utilized, directing them towards productive investment rather than distribution, which can have lasting impacts on economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: One-quarter of corporate profits over certain level to be transferred to special investment fund with Central Bank and to be used only for productive investment and by agreement with employee representatives.
